title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Mississippi
description: This race is for Mississippi’s Class II seat in the U.S. Senate, currently held by Republican Cindy Hyde-Smith. It matters because it will help determine control of the Senate in the 120th Congress, and Mississippi is widely rated as a solid Republican state, making the general election more about candidate contrast than overall partisan balance. The contest features an incumbent Republican, a Democratic district attorney, and an independent candidate, with themes centered on jobs, healthcare, fiscal policy, and public safety.
polling_note: No public polling found for this race as of 2026-04-01.

candidates.name: Cindy Hyde-Smith
candidates.summary: Cindy Hyde-Smith is the incumbent U.S. senator from Mississippi, first taking office in 2018. Before the Senate, she served as Mississippi commissioner of agriculture and commerce and in the state legislature. Her campaign and official Senate materials present her as a conservative aligned with Republican priorities.

candidates.name: Scott Colom
candidates.summary: Scott Colom is the Democratic nominee for U.S. Senate in Mississippi and currently serves as district attorney for Mississippi’s 16th Judicial District. His campaign emphasizes lowering costs, protecting hospitals, and bringing jobs to the state, while highlighting his record as a prosecutor and his earlier judicial nomination by President Biden. He is running as a reform-minded Democrat in a state that remains strongly Republican at the federal level.

candidates.name: Ty Pinkins
candidates.summary: Ty Pinkins is an independent candidate for U.S. Senate in Mississippi and previously ran for the seat in 2024. He is a U.S. Army veteran and attorney whose campaign focuses on housing, education, healthcare, and jobs, and he has framed his candidacy as a challenge to the state’s two-party system. Pinkins has also run for statewide office before, including Mississippi secretary of state in 2023.

candidates.name: Sarah Adlakha
candidates.summary: Sarah Adlakha ran in the Republican primary for U.S. Senate in Mississippi and is a physician by profession. Her campaign emphasized healthcare, affordability, education, border security, fiscal restraint, and ethics reform, presenting her as an outsider with experience in medicine and business. She lost the Republican primary to incumbent Cindy Hyde-Smith.
